content:
- Followed task lifecycle for story-secure-explicit-node-config task 06 (full verification).
- Deep-skeptical-reviewed the existing plan (TO BE VERIFIED -> NOW EXECUTE) and amended it:
  - added preflight env/disk capture, explicit RUST_TEST_THREADS guard, reordered gates to run make lint before make test-long, and wrapped make test-long with external timeout to prevent indefinite hangs.
- Executed gates with evidence logs under .ralph/evidence/story-secure-explicit-node-config/06-task-full-verification-for-secure-explicit-config-refactor/:
  - make check: pass
  - make lint: pass
  - make test: pass
  - make test-long: pass
- Updated task 06 markdown to status done/passes true/passing true; ticked acceptance criteria; added execution_report pointing to evidence dir.
- Ran /bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh (updated current_tasks.md, appended task_history, removed current_task.txt).
- Committed and pushed:
  - task finished 06...: all gates green (forced-added ignored evidence logs via git add -f).
  - follow-up commit: recorded learnings in AGENTS.md (make test rejects RUST_TEST_THREADS=1; evidence dir is gitignored so use git add -f).
- Next: choose the next task via the usual .ralph/current_task workflow.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and searched for any failing tasks (<passes>false</passes> / <passing>false</passing>) and meta-tasks.
- Found no failing bug tasks; found two non-bug tasks still <passes>false</passes> (docs verification task 33; logging file-sink backlog task 39-backlog).
- Per rules, chose the most logical next failing task: docs facts/writing verification (task 33) and wrote it to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Next: execute task 33 (deep skeptical docs verification) and update its pass status when complete.

- Subagent task: mapped authoritative code evidence for docs verification on DCS keyspace, trust/quorum semantics, startup planner, HA lifecycle, and fencing.
- Collected canonical symbol locations and line anchors in src/dcs/{keys,state,store,worker}.rs, src/runtime/node.rs, src/ha/{state,decide,actions,worker}.rs, plus tests/e2e in src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs and tests/bdd_api_http.rs.
- Next: hand back a structured evidence checklist grouped by requested topic with key types/functions/tests and absolute file links.

- Subagent scan task: reviewed docs/src/interfaces/*.md and docs/src/operations/*.md for factual claims.
- Mapped each major claim to concrete code/test evidence paths (API routes, authz logic, CLI command wiring, config parser/validator, runtime worker wiring, Makefile docs commands).
- Identified drift risks/ambiguous wording hotspots: Node API page omits fallback/debug routes while claiming API is “small”; deployment page states data-dir permissions/socket-path caveats as universal ops facts though enforced mostly in harness/tests; operations index has normative statements that should cite HA decision logic.
- Next (for parent agent): convert this into the verification checklist artifact and tighten wording in docs where claims overreach or hide scope.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt -> task 33 deep skeptical docs verification.
- Inspected docs tree under docs/src and SUMMARY.md (architecture/concepts/interfaces/operations/testing pages).
- Ran an rg command with backticks in double quotes and hit shell command substitution parse error; reran with single-quoted regex per workspace guidance.
- Spawned parallel subagents to (a) scan docs pages for high-risk factual claims + terminology drift, (b) scan interfaces/operations claims + drift risks, (c) locate authoritative code evidence for DCS keyspace, trust/quorum semantics, startup planner, HA lifecycle, fencing, API/CLI surfaces, and (d) propose a strict editorial rubric + verification artifact shape.
- Wrote a detailed Draft 1 execution plan into task 33 markdown, including: preflight evidence capture, creation of a committed verification report template under docs/src/verification/, page-by-page claim->evidence mapping method, diagram validation method, writing-quality rubric, explicit uncertainty logging + follow-up task creation, and full gate run list (make docs-build, make lint, make check, make test, make test-long) with evidence logs.
- Appended 'TO BE VERIFIED' at end of plan as required and stopped before any execution.

- Reviewed task 33 plan deeply for methodology quality, not implementation quality.
- Validated the plan text with line numbers and compared plan inventory vs actual docs/src tree and SUMMARY.
- Found hard gap: plan inventory omits existing docs files (architecture.md, components.md, glossary.md, operations.md), creating blind spots and false confidence risk.
- Identified unclear definitions in plan: "major claim", "verified", and write-quality pass criteria are partly subjective and not machine-checkable.
- Identified overreach/time-waste risk: mandatory full make check/test/test-long for docs methodology review without staged fast-fail can burn time and still not prove claim-level correctness.
- Prepared concrete improvements: expand coverage source to include all docs/src markdown + orphan checks, switch to claim manifest with IDs and evidence with line refs, add automation scripts for coverage/completeness and heuristic language checks, and tighten gate sequencing.
- Next if asked: patch task 33 file directly with these methodology improvements and add helper scripts.

- Scanned docs inventory under docs/src and compared three sets: filesystem markdown files, docs/src/SUMMARY.md links, and task 33 page checklist paths.
- Confirmed SUMMARY-linked pages align with task 33 checklist; all Mermaid-bearing pages are covered by task 33 (no mermaid page missing from the plan list).
- Found four markdown files present on disk but absent from SUMMARY and task 33 checklist: docs/src/architecture.md, docs/src/components.md, docs/src/glossary.md, docs/src/operations.md. Inspected contents: all are short "(moved)" redirect stubs with no Mermaid blocks.
- Found one task-33 path not on disk yet: docs/src/verification/task-33-docs-verification-report.md (expected artifact to be created during execution).
- Need to suggest plan corrections: explicitly include redirect-stub disposition (either verify as retained redirects or delete), add SUMMARY.md integrity verification step, and clarify verification-report file is output artifact (not pre-existing inventory input).

- Verified task 33 requested targets against Makefile and dry-run output: docs-build, lint, check, test, test-long all exist and map to concrete commands.
- Confirmed docs-build prerequisites are strict local binaries in .tools/mdbook/bin: mdbook + mdbook-mermaid; docs/book.toml config requires mermaid preprocessor command `mdbook-mermaid`.
- Confirmed test prerequisites include timeout/gtimeout binary and that make test hard-fails when RUST_TEST_THREADS=1; make test also skips ULTRA_LONG_TESTS and applies a hard 120s timeout around cargo test.
- Confirmed make test-long runs each ULTRA_LONG_TESTS case individually (currently 4 tests) without built-in timeout, so external timeout wrapper in plan is reasonable.
- Confirmed real-binary tests rely on .tools/postgres16/bin/* and .tools/etcd/bin/etcd; installers exist at tools/install-postgres16.sh and tools/install-etcd.sh, with platform caveats (postgres installer is AlmaLinux/RHEL-like only).
- Suggested plan corrections: add explicit prerequisite install/check step before gates; add explicit `unset RUST_TEST_THREADS`; keep make docs-build target name (not mdbook build directly); keep external timeout around make test-long.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and opened task 33 markdown.
- Detected task 33 plan was in TO BE VERIFIED state; performed deep skeptical plan review using 3 parallel explorer subagents (tooling/gates validation, docs inventory completeness scan, methodology critique).
- Updated the task 33 execution plan to close major confidence leaks:
  - strengthened the definition of “Verified” to require claim_id + doc line location + line-level evidence; forbade verifying absence claims without explicit guards/tests.
  - added Phase 0.5 prerequisites to install required binaries (mdbook, mdbook-mermaid, etcd, postgres16) and hard sanity checks (no optional tests).
  - added mechanical corpus coverage requirements (all docs/src/**/*.md dispositioned, SUMMARY integrity rules) and explicitly added legacy redirect stub files that were previously omitted.
  - captured high-risk word grep + mermaid page list into evidence; refined “In one minute” requirement to apply only to long explanatory pages.
  - added make docs-hygiene gate and made test/test-long commands more robust (unset RUST_TEST_THREADS; portable timeout/gtimeout wrapper).
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE at end of task 33 plan; did not start execution.

- Next: on the next context window, read task 33 markdown (NOW EXECUTE) and execute the plan as written (no more exploring), ticking checkboxes as steps are completed.
